steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two sculptures. The relevant facts from the notes: Kosuth’s sculpture spells out a phrase; Dawood’s forms an abstract moon shape. A good answer must make that contrast clear and central — nothing else.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Molded into an evocative phrase in Catharsis, Conversion, Traumata (1986), an abstract form in Harvest Moon (2013), and an advertising tool in decades past, neon lighting has a number of different applications.’: buries the difference between the two sculptures inside a three-part list about advertising history. The goal is to emphasize the difference, not list applications of neon. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘While the sculptures share a common material, one spells out a phrase while the other is decidedly more abstract.’: directly states the key difference between the two works in a single, focused sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Kosuth's sculpture was made in 1986 and Dawood's in 2013, both well after the advertising heyday of neon signs had passed.’: emphasizes when the sculptures were made, not how they differ visually or conceptually. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘American artist Joseph Kosuth's and British/Pakistani artist Shezad Dawood's sculptures have employed neon signs in different ways, demonstrating the versatility of the popular material.’: gestures at a difference but concludes with the artists’ shared use of neon, softening the contrast rather than emphasizing it. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
